 {"id":"bd-eyrh","title":"🤝 HANDOFF: Review remaining beads PRs","description":"## Current State
Just merged PR #653 (doctor refactor) and added tests to restore coverage.

## Remaining Open PRs to Review
Run `gh pr list --repo steveyegge/beads` to see current list. As of handoff:

1. #655 - feat: Linear Integration (jblwilliams)
2. #651 - feat(audit): agent audit trail (dchichkov)
3. #648 - Stop init creating redundant @AGENTS.md (maphew)
4. #646 - fix(unix): handle Statfs field types (jordanhubbard)
5. #645 - feat: /plan-to-beads Claude Code command (petebytes)
6. #642, #641, #640 - sync branch fixes (cpdata)

## Review Checklist
- Check CI status with `gh pr checks <num> --repo steveyegge/beads`
- Verify no .beads/ data leaking (we have a hook now)
- Review code quality
- Merge good ones, request changes on problematic ones

## Notes
- User wants us to be proactive about merging good PRs
- Can add tests ourselves if coverage drops","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"message","assignee":"beads-dave","created_at":"2025-12-19T17:44:34.149837-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T17:53:20.386937-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T17:53:20.386937-08:00","sender":"Steve Yegge","ephemeral":true}
 {"id":"bd-eyto","title":"Time-dependent tests may be flaky near TTL boundary","description":"Several tombstone merge tests use time.Now() to create test data: time.Now().Add(-24 * time.Hour), time.Now().Add(-60 * 24 * time.Hour), etc. While these work reliably in practice (24h vs 30d TTL has large margin), they could theoretically be flaky if: 1) Tests run slowly, 2) System clock changes during test, 3) TTL constants change. Recommendation: Consider using a fixed reference time or time injection for deterministic tests. Lower priority since current margin is large. Files: internal/merge/merge_test.go:1337-1338, 1352-1353, 1548-1549, 1590-1591","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-05T16:37:02.348143-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-05T16:37:02.348143-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-fi05","title":"bd sync fails with orphaned issues and duplicate ID conflict","description":"After fixing the deleted_at TEXT column scanning bug (commit 18b1eb2), bd sync still fails with two issues:

1. Orphan Detection Warning: 12 orphaned child issues whose parents no longer exist (bd-cb64c226.* and bd-cbed9619.*)

2. Import Failure: UNIQUE constraint failed for bd-360 - this tombstone exists in both DB and JSONL

Error: "Import failed: error creating depth-0 issues: bulk insert issues: failed to insert issue bd-360: sqlite3: constraint failed: UNIQUE constraint failed: issues.id"

Fix options:
- Delete orphaned child issues with bd delete
- Resolve bd-360 duplicate (in deletions.jsonl vs tombstone in DB)
- Reset sync branch: git branch -f beads-sync main && git push --force-with-lease origin beads-sync","notes":"Fixed tombstone constraint violation bug. When deleting closed issues, the CHECK constraint (status = 'closed') = (closed_at IS NOT NULL) was violated because CreateTombstone didn't clear closed_at. Fix: set closed_at = NULL in tombstone creation SQL.

The sync data corruption (orphaned issues in beads-sync branch) requires manual cleanup: reset sync branch with 'git branch -f beads-sync main && git push --force-with-lease origin beads-sync'","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-13T07:14:33.831346-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T10:50:48.545465-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T07:30:33.843986-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-fom","title":"Remove all deletions.jsonl code except migration","description":"There's deletions manifest code spread across the entire codebase that should have been removed after tombstone migration:

Files with deletions code (non-migration):
- internal/deletions/ - entire package
- cmd/bd/sync.go - 25+ references, auto-compact, sanitize
- cmd/bd/delete.go - dual-writes to deletions.jsonl
- internal/importer/importer.go - checks deletions manifest
- internal/syncbranch/worktree.go - merges deletions.jsonl
- cmd/bd/doctor/fix/sync.go - cleanupDeletionsManifest
- cmd/bd/doctor/fix/deletions.go - HydrateDeletionsManifest
- cmd/bd/integrity.go - checks deletions for data loss
- cmd/bd/deleted.go - entire command
- cmd/bd/compact.go - pruneDeletionsManifest
- cmd/bd/doctor.go - checkDeletionsManifest
- Plus many more

Action: Aggressively remove all non-migration deletions code. Tombstones are the only deletion mechanism now.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T13:29:04.960863-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"bd-fu83","title":"Fix daemon/direct mode inconsistency in relate and duplicate commands","description":"The relate.go and duplicate.go commands have inconsistent daemon/direct mode handling:

When daemonClient is connected, they resolve IDs via RPC but then perform updates directly via store.UpdateIssue(), bypassing the daemon.

Affected locations:
- relate.go:125-139 (runRelate update)
- relate.go:235-246 (runUnrelate update)  
- duplicate.go:120 (runDuplicate update)
- duplicate.go:207 (runSupersede update)

Should either use RPC for updates when daemon is running, or document why direct access is intentional.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T20:52:54.164189-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T20:52:54.164189-08:00"}
@@ -193,7 +192,6 @@
 {"id":"bd-ola6","title":"Implement transaction retry logic for SQLITE_BUSY","description":"BEGIN IMMEDIATE fails immediately on SQLITE_BUSY instead of retrying with exponential backoff.

Location: internal/storage/sqlite/sqlite.go:223-225

Problem:
- Under concurrent write load, BEGIN IMMEDIATE can fail with SQLITE_BUSY
- Current implementation fails immediately instead of retrying
- Results in spurious failures under normal concurrent usage

Solution: Implement exponential backoff retry:
- Retry up to N times (e.g., 5)
- Backoff: 10ms, 20ms, 40ms, 80ms, 160ms
- Check for context cancellation between retries
- Only retry on SQLITE_BUSY/database locked errors

Impact: Spurious failures under concurrent write load

Effort: 3 hours","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-16T14:51:31.247147-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-16T14:51:31.247147-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-ork0","title":"Add comments to 30+ silently ignored errors or fix them","description":"Code health review found 30+ instances of error suppression using blank identifier without explanation:

Good examples (with comments):
- merge.go: _ = gitRmCmd.Run() // Ignore errors
- daemon_watcher.go: _ = watcher.Add(...) // Ignore error

Bad examples (no context):
- create.go:213: dbPrefix, _ = store.GetConfig(ctx, "issue_prefix")
- daemon_sync_branch.go: _ = daemonClient.Close()
- migrate_hash_ids.go, version_tracking.go: _ = store.Close()

Fix: Add comments explaining WHY errors are ignored, or handle them properly.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:17:25.899372-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T18:17:25.899372-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ork0","depends_on_id":"bd-tggf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:19:06.275843-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"bd-otf4","title":"Code Review: PR #481 - Context Engineering Optimizations","description":"Comprehensive code review of the merged context engineering PR (PR #481) that reduces MCP context usage by 80-90%.

## Summary
The PR successfully implements lazy tool schema loading and minimal issue models to reduce context window usage. Overall implementation is solid and well-tested.

## Positive Findings
✅ Well-designed models (IssueMinimal, CompactedResult)
✅ Comprehensive test coverage (28 tests, all passing)
✅ Clear documentation and comments
✅ Backward compatibility preserved (show() still returns full Issue)
✅ Sensible defaults (COMPACTION_THRESHOLD=20, PREVIEW_COUNT=5)
✅ Tool catalog complete with all 15 tools documented

## Issues Identified
See linked issues for specific followup tasks.

## Context Engineering Architecture
- discover_tools(): List tool names only (~500 bytes vs ~15KB)
- get_tool_info(name): Get specific tool details on-demand
- IssueMinimal: Lightweight model for list views (~80 bytes vs ~400 bytes)
- CompactedResult: Auto-compacts results with >20 issues
- _to_minimal(): Conversion function (efficient, no N+1 issues)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-14T14:24:13.523532-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-14T14:24:13.523532-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-z86n","title":"Code Review: PR #551 - Persist close_reason to issues table","description":"Code review of PR #551 which fixes close_reason persistence bug.

## Summary
The PR correctly fixes a bug where close_reason was only stored in the events table, not in the issues.close_reason column. This caused `bd show --json` to return empty close_reason.

## What Was Fixed
- ✅ CloseIssue now updates both close_reason and closed_at
- ✅ ReOpenIssue clears both close_reason and closed_at
- ✅ Comprehensive tests added for both storage and CLI layers
- ✅ Clear documentation in queries.go about dual storage strategy

## Quality Assessment
✅ Tests cover both storage layer and CLI JSON output
✅ Handles reopen case (clearing close_reason)
✅ Good comments explaining dual-storage design
✅ No known issues

## Potential Followups
See linked issues for suggestions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:06.887069-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:06.887069-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-z8a6","title":"bd delete --from-file should add deleted issues to deletions manifest","description":"When using bd delete --from-file to bulk delete issues, the deleted issue IDs are not being added to the deletions.jsonl manifest.

This causes those issues to be resurrected during bd sync when git history scanning finds them in old commits.

Expected: All deleted issues should be added to deletions.jsonl so they wont be reimported from git history.

Workaround: Manually add deletion records to deletions.jsonl.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T01:48:14.099855-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}
